question.stem: A student team is building a campus event reminder app and two teammates changed the same feature in incompatible ways. Which collaboration practice most directly reduces this risk?
choices: Let each teammate keep private copies only
Use shared version control with clear commit messages
Merge files only on presentation day
question.explanation: Version control and clear history support coordinated collaboration. A common wrong choice is "Let each teammate keep private copies only" because it sounds plausible but does not address the core condition. Takeaway: identify the exact requirement before selecting an approach.
